1.11a: Address for standard documents: Customer Support Department, Sentinel House, Morton Road, Darlington, DL1 4PT.
1.11b: Contact point for inappropriate standard documents: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
1.7: Lending is not permitted where the borrower and mortgagor are different entities.
1.14: Solicitor may not act if they or an immediate family member is the seller.
1.15: Solicitor may not act if they or an immediate family member is the borrower.
3.1.3: Notification of seller's conveyancer details is not required.
Notification in writing is required if a conflict of interest exists or the same firm acts for both parties.
3.1.4: Contact for unrepresented sellers: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
3.1.5: No alternative identity verification documents are accepted.
3.2.3: Seller's solicitor details not required unless a conflict of interest exists or the same firm acts for both parties.
3.2.4: Contact for unrepresented sellers: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
4.1: A copy of the valuation report is provided with the mortgage offer.
4.3: Contact for incorrect valuation assumptions: Mortgage Processing Team, Principal Office.
4.5a: Contact for re-inspections: Mortgage Processing Team, Principal Office.
4.5b: Certificate of Title destination: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
5.1.1: Properties sold within the last 6 months are generally unacceptable, excluding part-exchange from house builders.
Remortgages within 6 months of purchase are generally unacceptable unless due to inheritance or replacing bridging finance.
Capital raising is prohibited for remortgages replacing bridging finance or cash purchases, except for refurbishment costs.
Sub-sales, back-to-back transactions, and assignable contracts are prohibited.
Granting a lease immediately after acquiring freehold for the purpose of a sale is considered a prohibited sub-sale.
5.2.1: Contact if seller is not the registered owner: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are not required.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted for remortgages only.
Search firms must subscribe to the Search Code and be monitored by the PCCB.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted for remortgages only.
Minimum insurance cover must equal the open market value of the property.
5.5.3a: Contact for breach of planning/building conditions: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
5.5.3b: Original planning permissions are not required; borrowers should retain them.
5.5.3c: Building regulation consents are not required; borrowers should retain them.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use are not required; borrowers should retain them.
5.5.4: Contact for restrictions affecting marketability: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are generally not accepted; refer cases to Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
5.7.1c: Contact for freehold flats/flying freeholds: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
5.8.1: Converted buildings of 4 or fewer flats where the borrower owns the freehold and occupies a flat are not accepted.
5.8.5: Cross-over freehold/leasehold arrangements for two-flat buildings are not accepted.
5.10.1: Contact for usage restrictions: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
5.13.1: Contact for secondary charges or non-funded purchase balances: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
5.14.9: Regulated leases under the Leasehold Reform (Ground Rent) Act 2022 must be peppercorn rent.
Unregulated leases: Ground rent must be reasonable and not exceed 0.1% of market value.
Ground rent increases linked to indices other than RPI are unacceptable.
Ground rent multipliers (e.g., doubling) are unacceptable.
Leases potentially treated as ASTs under Housing Act 1988 (ground rent >£250 or >£1000 in London) require a lease variation or indemnity insurance.
5.14.10: Contact for service charge matters: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
5.14.11: Indemnity insurance is accepted for unsatisfactory lease terms.
5.14.12: Lender does not require ground rent/service charge receipts; borrower to retain.
5.14.13: Lender does not require receipted notice of service; borrower to retain.
5.14.15a: Contact for absentee/insolvent landlords: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance for absent/insolvent landlords is accepted subject to case-by-referral before Certificate of Title.
5.15.2a: Contact for management company issues: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
5.15.2b: Lender does not require management company share certificates; borrower to retain.
5.15.2c: Lender does not require signed blank stock transfer forms; borrower to retain.
5.15.2d: Lender does not require memorandum and articles of association; borrower to retain.
5.16.2: Contact if unable to certify search entries: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
5.17.5a: Copy of Power of Attorney must be sent with the Certificate of Title.
5.17.5b: Statutory declaration of non-revocation must be sent after completion.
5.19.1: Contact for affordable housing/shared ownership: Commercial Lending Department, Principal Office.
5.20.1: Report if solar PV panel roof space leases do not meet UK Finance minimum requirements.
5.20.3: Solar PV installers must be MCS certified.
Lender requires planning permissions and structural surveys for solar installations.
5.20.4: Green Deal plans must be disclosed; provide EPC with 22-digit reference or provider contact details.
6.1.3: Contact if mortgage offer not taken up: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
6.2.1: Contact for property description discrepancies: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
6.3.1: Contact for purchase price issues: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
6.4.4: All incentives, allowances, or discounts must be reported to the Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
6.4.5: Contact if conveyancer lacks control over purchase funds: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
6.5.1: Contact if vacant possession is not provided: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
6.6.1: Contact for let properties: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
6.6.2: Contact for missing letting details: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
6.6.3: Certified copy of tenancy agreement required after completion.
6.6.4: Lending on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO) is prohibited.
6.7.1: Accepted warranty schemes: NHBC, LABC New Home Warranty Premier Guarantee, Castle 10, Build-Zone, Protek, Q Assure, AEDIS, Premier, ICW, One Guarantee, Advantage Home Construction Insurance, ARK Insurance Group, BLP (excluding self-build), ABC+, Checkmate, CADIS.
6.7.2: Lender requires a copy of the new home warranty documentation; original is retained by borrower.
6.7.3: Building standards indemnity scheme assignments are not required; borrower to retain.
6.7.4: Properties without a warranty scheme are unacceptable unless monitored by a qualified Professional Consultant with PI insurance; refer to Customer Support.
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificate must be sent prior to exchange of contracts.
6.8.1: Contact for unadopted road/sewer issues: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
6.9.1: Contact for missing easements: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
6.10.2: Retentions or instalments released to solicitor or borrower.
6.11.1: Contact for redevelopment or road proposals: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
6.12.1: Contact for pre-emption rights or resale restrictions: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
6.13.1: Contact for improvement/repair grants: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
7.3: Consent to mortgage required from all occupants aged 17 or over prior to completion.
7.4: Contact for accuracy of disclosed information: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
8.1: Solicitor may not advise specified third parties.
9.1: Indemnity insurance policy document not required; insurer and policy number must be noted on Certificate of Title.
9.2: Indemnity limit must be the higher of the property value or purchase price.
10.2a: Mortgage advance paid via electronic transfer.
10.2b: Minimum notice for funds: 48 hours (if all conditions satisfied).
10.3: No standard deductions are made from the advance unless specified in the Offer.
10.7: On delayed completion, funds may be retained for up to 7 days.
If delay exceeds 7 days, funds must be returned by EFT immediately; confirm via telephone and follow up with fax/email.
10.9: Contact for delayed completion: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
10.10: Funds may be held for a maximum of 7 days.
10.11: Lender reserves the right to charge the mortgage interest rate if return of advance is delayed.
12.3.1: Contact for release of retentions/instalments: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
14.1.4: Form CH2 application is not required.
14.1.5: Lender does not require original mortgage deeds or title documents.
14.2.1: Title deeds and documents are to be retained by the borrower, except for the TID.
14.2.2: Title Information Document (TID) must be sent to the lender after completion.
16.1.1: Contact for title documents: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
16.3.1: Lender does not have a standard form of transfer or deed of covenant.
16.3.2: Contact for debt amount: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
16.3.4: TR1 must be sent for execution prior to registration at the Land Registry.
16.3.7a: Contact for transfer of equity execution: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
16.3.7b: Attestation clause: 'This document was executed by authority of the Board of Directors of Darlington Building Society'.
16.4.1: Contact for consent to let applications: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
16.4.2: A copy of the proposed tenancy agreement must be sent to the lender.

16.5.3b: Deed of rectification destination: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
16.5.3c: Deed of easement destination: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
16.5.3d: Option agreements destination: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
17.1.1: Contact for redemption statements: Customer Support Department, Principal Office.
Reason for redemption (sale, remortgage, etc.) must be disclosed.
17.2.1a: Cheque repayments: 8 Tubwell Row Branch, Darlington, DL1 1NX.
17.2.1b: Discharge is processed electronically via DS1 through Land Registry Direct.
